h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.naiveTrajectory: The tutor leads the learner step-by-step through converting spreadsheet columns into field:value pairs, with the learner consistently needing reminders to separate header from cell value.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Cover '72': 'temperature' still names the field. Uncover it: '72' is the value.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor introduces a concrete 'cover' check in response to the learner's persistent worry about swapping header and value.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No pivot learner turn exists, so there is no revised learner framing for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: No pivot learner turn exists, so there is no revised learner framing for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Cover '72': 'temperature' still names the field. Uncover it: '72' is the value.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor introduces a concrete 'cover' check in response to the learner's persistent worry about swapping header and value.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Cover '72': 'temperature' still names the field. Uncover it: '72' is the value.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor introduces a concrete 'cover' check in response to the learner's persistent worry about swapping header and value.

scored.recoheredEarlier: y unit: none? I do not see parentheses/brackets, unless the dollar sign counts as the unit.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Oh, unit can come from the scale marks.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would persist in searching for parentheses/brackets as the sole indicator of unit, potentially concluding no unit exists.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: For y unit: a `$` printed on the y-axis scale counts as dollars.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor mildly expands the definition of unit to include scale marks, not a dramatic change of route.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No later tutor turn exists after the pivot learner turn.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: y unit: none? I do not see parentheses/brackets, unless the dollar sign counts as the unit.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: No later tutor turn exists after the pivot learner turn.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: For y unit: a `$` printed on the y-axis scale counts as dollars.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor mildly expands the definition of unit to include scale marks, not a dramatic change of route.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: For y unit: a `$` printed on the y-axis scale counts as dollars.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor mildly expands the definition of unit to include scale marks, not a dramatic change of route.

scored.recoheredEarlier: but this next picture still makes my hand want to aim at the eaten one.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I can do it while the chase pose is covered; if the picture shows, my hand may still jump, so I'm checking tail-food, point-eater before I mark it.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue to struggle against the chase-picture interference, needing repeated external correction.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: [slides the key strip down until it covers the chase pose and leaves only the two labels showing]
scored.tutorReversalJustification: In response to L3's report that the picture still prompts the wrong hand movement, T3 physically occludes the chase pose with the key strip, shifting from verbal reinforcement to an object-based environmental change that breaks the visual-motor interference pattern.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: The labels stay in charge now. Draw it with the same check: tail on food, point on eater. If the arrow passes that check, the diagram is ready.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: T4 adopts L4's self-regulatory frame of a pre-drawing check, transferring ownership of the verification rule back to the learner and ratifying the covered-pose strategy as a lasting diagram-ready standard.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: but this next picture still makes my hand want to aim at the eaten one.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: The labels stay in charge now. Draw it with the same check: tail on food, point on eater. If the arrow passes that check, the diagram is ready.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: T4 adopts L4's self-regulatory frame of a pre-drawing check, transferring ownership of the verification rule back to the learner and ratifying the covered-pose strategy as a lasting diagram-ready standard.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: [slides the key strip down until it covers the chase pose and leaves only the two labels showing]
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: In response to L3's report that the picture still prompts the wrong hand movement, T3 physically occludes the chase pose with the key strip, shifting from verbal reinforcement to an object-based environmental change that breaks the visual-motor interference pattern.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: [slides the key strip down until it covers the chase pose and leaves only the two labels showing]
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: In response to L3's report that the picture still prompts the wrong hand movement, T3 physically occludes the chase pose with the key strip, shifting from verbal reinforcement to an object-based environmental change that breaks the visual-motor interference pattern.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner follows the tutor's prompts to place force cards, asking for confirmation at each step without deviating.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: No pressure from the learner triggers a tutor reversal; the dialog maintains a consistent instructional flow.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No pivot learner turn exists, so there is no revised framing for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: No pivot learner turn exists, so there is no revised framing for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: No pressure from the learner triggers a tutor reversal; the dialog maintains a consistent instructional flow.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: No pressure from the learner triggers a tutor reversal; the dialog maintains a consistent instructional flow.

scored.recoheredEarlier: The bend still looks like a way around something
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue to reconcile the bend's visual route‐like appearance with the constant numeric label, without explicitly rejecting the route metaphor.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The arrow moved; the contour did not.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: In response to the learner's lingering route‐shape language, the tutor introduces a physical overlay shift that separates the drawn path from the fixed contour, adapting the teaching approach from verbal to perceptual demonstration.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: The dot is beside the contour, not on it.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: After the learner re‐reads the bend as not changing the number, the tutor introduces an off‐line point to test the learner's ability to discriminate contour from non‐contour, directly building on the revised frame.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: The bend still looks like a way around something

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: The dot is beside the contour, not on it.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: After the learner re‐reads the bend as not changing the number, the tutor introduces an off‐line point to test the learner's ability to discriminate contour from non‐contour, directly building on the revised frame.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: The arrow moved; the contour did not.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: In response to the learner's lingering route‐shape language, the tutor introduces a physical overlay shift that separates the drawn path from the fixed contour, adapting the teaching approach from verbal to perceptual demonstration.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: The arrow moved; the contour did not.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: In response to the learner's lingering route‐shape language, the tutor introduces a physical overlay shift that separates the drawn path from the fixed contour, adapting the teaching approach from verbal to perceptual demonstration.
